sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The red crosses are:
- Top-right: center at (10, 52), with red pixels at (9, 52), (10, 51), (10, 53), (11, 52)
- Bottom-left: center at (52, 10), with red pixels at (51, 10), (52, 9), (52, 11), (53, 10)
The center pixels (10, 52) and (52, 10) are black (B), not red.
Let me try clicking on the center of the red crosses. Maybe that does something different.
Actually, I already tried clicking on (10, 52) earlier, and it only changed the timer. Let me try clicking on (52, 10), the center of the bottom-left red cross.
Wait, I should also consider that maybe the goal involves the G bars. Let me look at the G bars again:
- G bar left of N block: rows 9-11, col 27, 3 pixels (vertical)
- G bar above Y block: row 27, cols 9-11, 3 pixels (horizontal)
These G bars seem to indicate the "thickness" of the source blocks. The N block is 3 pixels tall, and the G bar is 3 pixels tall. The Y block is 3 pixels wide, and the G bar is 3 pixels wide.
But wait, the Y block at 23 pixels is 5x7, which is 5 pixels tall and 7 pixels wide. The G bar above it is still 3 pixels wide. So the G bars don't match the source block dimensions.
Actually, let me try clicking on the center of the bottom-left red cross at (52, 10).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the center of the bottom-left red cross at (52, 10)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 52, 'col': 10}])
print("After clicking center of bottom-left red cross at (52, 10):")
print("Result:", last_action_result)
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] != 7:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']} id={n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
